chi-square for all variables

# libs
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(patchwork)   # to combine multiple plots
library(dplyr)
```

# load data
```{r}
ps <- readRDS('./RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v3.rds')
ps
bad_batch_samples <- c('B-024-1', 'B-024-2', 'B-027-2','B-027-1','B-030-1', 'B-030-2')

if (length(bad_batch_samples) > 0) {
  ps <- prune_samples(!(sample_names(ps) %in% bad_batch_samples), ps)
  cat("Removed", length(bad_batch_samples), "samples\n")
}
ps@sam_data$Batch <- as.factor(ps@sam_data$Batch)
ps = prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps) > 10, ps)
level <- 'Species'
ps <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE)
det <- 1/100
prev <- 10/100
# Prepare the phyloseq object
taxas <- core_members(ps, detection = det, prevalence = prev, include.lowest = F)
ps <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps)
ps_meta <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps))

# Find categorical variables (factor or character)
categorical_vars <- names(ps_meta)[sapply(ps_meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]

# Remove variables we don't want to stratify by
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, 
                            c('Age', 'Group', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 'Weight', 'Timepoint', 'SampleID'))
# After your existing phyloseq preparation code (ps, ps_meta already exist)

# Make sure Group is a factor
ps_meta$Group <- as.factor(ps_meta$Group)
group_var <- "Group"
# Make sure Timepoint is a factor
ps_meta$Timepoint <- as.factor(ps_meta$Timepoint)
timepoint_var <- "Timepoint"

# Identify categorical variables (excluding Group itself and unwanted columns)
categorical_vars <- names(ps_meta)[sapply(ps_meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, 
                            c('Age', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 'Weight', 'Timepoint', 'SampleID', group_var))

# Remove variables with only one level (no variation)
categorical_vars <- categorical_vars[sapply(ps_meta[, categorical_vars, drop = FALSE], 
                                            function(x) length(unique(x)) > 1)]
```

# analysis loop and plots by Group
```{r}
# After preparing ps_meta as a data.frame (or tibble)
# Ensure Group is a factor
ps_meta$Group <- as.factor(ps_meta$Group)
group_var <- "Group"

# Categorical variables (excluding unwanted)
categorical_vars <- names(ps_meta)[sapply(ps_meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, 
                            c('Age', 'Height', 'No_Patient', 'Weight', 'Timepoint', 'SampleID', group_var))
categorical_vars <- categorical_vars[sapply(ps_meta[, categorical_vars, drop = FALSE], 
                                            function(x) length(unique(x)) > 1)]

# Results data frame
results <- data.frame(Variable = character(),
                      Test = character(),
                      Statistic = numeric(),
                      df = integer(),
                      p_value = numeric(),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Loop with [[ ]] fix
for (var in categorical_vars) {
  x_vec <- ps_meta[[var]]
  y_vec <- ps_meta[[group_var]]
  tab <- table(x_vec, y_vec, useNA = "no")
  if (any(dim(tab) < 2) || sum(tab) < 2) next
  
  chi_temp <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
  if (any(chi_temp$expected < 5)) {
    ft <- fisher.test(tab, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Variable = var,
                                         Test = "Fisher's exact",
                                         Statistic = NA,
                                         df = NA,
                                         p_value = ft$p.value))
  } else {
    ct <- chisq.test(tab)
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Variable = var,
                                         Test = "Chi-square",
                                         Statistic = ct$statistic,
                                         df = ct$parameter,
                                         p_value = ct$p.value))
  }
}

# Adjust p-values
results$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results$p_value, method = "BH")
results <- results[order(results$p_adjusted), ]
print(subset(results, p_adjusted < 0.05))

# Plotting (also use [[ ]])
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

group_colors <- c("A" = "red", "B" = "blue", "C" = "green")
sig_vars <- results$Variable[results$p_adjusted < 0.05]
plot_list <- list()


for (var in sig_vars) {
  plot_df <- data.frame(
    var_level = ps_meta[[var]],
    group = ps_meta[[group_var]]
  )
  plot_df <- plot_df[complete.cases(plot_df), ]
  
  prop_data <- as.data.frame(prop.table(table(plot_df$var_level, plot_df$group), margin = 1))
  colnames(prop_data) <- c(var, group_var, "Proportion")
  
  p_adj_val <- results$p_adjusted[results$Variable == var]
  p_label <- ifelse(p_adj_val < 0.001, "p_adj < 0.001", paste("p_adj =", round(p_adj_val, 4)))
  
  p <- ggplot(prop_data, aes_string(x = var, y = "Proportion", fill = group_var)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors, name = "Group") +
    labs(title = paste("Association with Group -", var),
         subtitle = p_label,
         x = var, y = "Proportion") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  plot_list[[var]] <- p
  
}

# Save as PDF (good for publication)
ggsave("chi_square_plots_vs_group.pdf", 
       plot = wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2), 
       width = 12, height = 8 * ceiling(length(plot_list)/2))

# Or save as PNG (good for quick viewing)
ggsave("chi_square_plots_vs_group.png", 
       plot = wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2), 
       width = 12, height = 8 * ceiling(length(plot_list)/2), dpi = 300)

if (length(plot_list) > 0) {
  wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2)
} else {
  cat("No significant associations found.\n")
}
# save resulting table
write.csv(results, "chi_square_results_vs_group.csv", row.names = FALSE)
sig_results <- subset(results, p_adjusted < 0.05)
write.csv(sig_results, "chi_square_significant_results_vs_group.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

# plots by Group
```{r}
# Create a folder (if it doesn't exist)
plot_folder <- "chi_square_plots"
if (!dir.exists(plot_folder)) dir.create(plot_folder)

# Loop over significant variables and save both plot types
for (var in sig_vars) {
  # ----- Side-by-side bar plot -----
  plot_df <- data.frame(
    var_level = ps_meta[[var]],
    group = ps_meta[[group_var]]
  )
  plot_df <- plot_df[complete.cases(plot_df), ]
  
  count_data <- as.data.frame(table(plot_df$var_level, plot_df$group))
  colnames(count_data) <- c(var, group_var, "Count")
  
  p_bar <- ggplot(count_data, aes_string(x = var, y = "Count", fill = group_var)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
    labs(title = paste(var, "vs Group"), 
         subtitle = paste("p_adj =", round(results$p_adjusted[results$Variable == var], 4))) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder, paste0(var, "_side_by_side.pdf")), 
         plot = p_bar, width = 8, height = 6)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder, paste0(var, "_side_by_side.png")), 
         plot = p_bar, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
  # ----- Heatmap of residuals -----
  tab <- table(ps_meta[[var]], ps_meta[[group_var]])
  chi <- chisq.test(tab)
  resid_df <- as.data.frame(chi$stdres)
  colnames(resid_df) <- c(var, group_var, "Residual")
  
  p_heat <- ggplot(resid_df, aes_string(x = var, y = group_var, fill = "Residual")) +
    geom_tile() +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "blue", 
                         midpoint = 0, name = "Std. Residual") +
    labs(title = paste(var, "vs", group_var),
         subtitle = paste("Chi-square p_adj =", round(results$p_adjusted[results$Variable == var], 4))) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder, paste0(var, "_residual_heatmap.pdf")), 
         plot = p_heat, width = 8, height = 6)
  ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder, paste0(var, "_residual_heatmap.png")), 
         plot = p_heat, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}

cat("Plots saved in folder:", plot_folder)
```

# by Timepoint analysis and plots
```{r}
results_time <- data.frame(Variable = character(),
                           Test = character(),
                           Statistic = numeric(),
                           df = integer(),
                           p_value = numeric(),
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

for (var in categorical_vars) {
  x_vec <- ps_meta[[var]]
  y_vec <- ps_meta[[timepoint_var]]
  tab <- table(x_vec, y_vec, useNA = "no")
  if (any(dim(tab) < 2) || sum(tab) < 2) next
  
  chi_temp <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
  if (any(chi_temp$expected < 5)) {
    ft <- fisher.test(tab, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)
    results_time <- rbind(results_time, data.frame(Variable = var,
                                                   Test = "Fisher's exact",
                                                   Statistic = NA,
                                                   df = NA,
                                                   p_value = ft$p.value))
  } else {
    ct <- chisq.test(tab)
    results_time <- rbind(results_time, data.frame(Variable = var,
                                                   Test = "Chi-square",
                                                   Statistic = ct$statistic,
                                                   df = ct$parameter,
                                                   p_value = ct$p.value))
  }
}

# Adjust p‑values
results_time$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_time$p_value, method = "BH")
results_time <- results_time[order(results_time$p_adjusted), ]

# Show significant associations
sig_time <- subset(results_time, p_adjusted < 0.05)
print(sig_time)
library(ggplot2)

# Create folder
plot_folder_time <- "chi_square_plots_timepoint"
if (!dir.exists(plot_folder_time)) dir.create(plot_folder_time)

# Define two colors for Timepoint (e.g., orange and purple)
time_colors <- c("-1" = "orange", "-2" = "purple")   # adjust level names if needed

# Get significant variables (or use all if you prefer)
sig_vars_time <- results_time$Variable[results_time$p_adjusted < 0.05]

if (length(sig_vars_time) > 0) {
  for (var in sig_vars_time) {
    # ----- Side‑by‑side bar plot -----
    plot_df <- data.frame(
      var_level = ps_meta[[var]],
      timepoint = ps_meta[[timepoint_var]]
    )
    plot_df <- plot_df[complete.cases(plot_df), ]
    
    count_data <- as.data.frame(table(plot_df$var_level, plot_df$timepoint))
    colnames(count_data) <- c(var, timepoint_var, "Count")
    
    p_bar <- ggplot(count_data, aes_string(x = var, y = "Count", fill = timepoint_var)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = time_colors) +
      labs(title = paste(var, "vs Timepoint"),
           subtitle = paste("p_adj =", round(results_time$p_adjusted[results_time$Variable == var], 4))) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    
    ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder_time, paste0(var, "_side_by_side.pdf")),
           plot = p_bar, width = 8, height = 6)
    ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder_time, paste0(var, "_side_by_side.png")),
           plot = p_bar, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    
    # ----- Heatmap of residuals -----
    tab <- table(ps_meta[[var]], ps_meta[[timepoint_var]])
    # Avoid chi-square on empty or degenerate tables
    if (all(dim(tab) >= 2) && sum(tab) > 0) {
      chi <- chisq.test(tab)
      resid_df <- as.data.frame(chi$stdres)
      colnames(resid_df) <- c(var, timepoint_var, "Residual")
      
      p_heat <- ggplot(resid_df, aes_string(x = var, y = timepoint_var, fill = "Residual")) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "blue",
                             midpoint = 0, name = "Std. Residual") +
        labs(title = paste(var, "vs", timepoint_var),
             subtitle = paste("p_adj =", round(results_time$p_adjusted[results_time$Variable == var], 4))) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
      
      ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder_time, paste0(var, "_residual_heatmap.pdf")),
             plot = p_heat, width = 8, height = 6)
      ggsave(filename = file.path(plot_folder_time, paste0(var, "_residual_heatmap.png")),
             plot = p_heat, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    }
  }
  cat("Plots saved in folder:", plot_folder_time, "\n")
} else {
  cat("No significant associations with Timepoint after multiple testing correction.\n")
}

# Save the full results table
write.csv(results_time, "chi_square_results_timepoint.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
